D9 Biology L4 Analyze Reference

Carotenoid Antenna — Fill the Green Gap

carotenoids absorb 400-500 nm (the blue-green band chlorophyll misses) and transfer energy to chlorophyll a — they're an antenna *and* a sunscreen against triplet oxygen damage

β-Carotene, lutein, zeaxanthin sit alongside chlorophyll in the light-harvesting complex LH2. Their long π-conjugation (~9-11 double bonds) absorbs 400-500 nm — the band chlorophyll a poorly covers. Excitation transfers via Förster resonance to chlorophyll a within picoseconds. Carotenoids also quench harmful chlorophyll triplet states (singlet-oxygen suppressant).

Toggle pigment combinations. The combined absorbance covers far more of the visible than chlorophyll alone — the antenna effect.
